refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall achieve and maintain operational availability of ≥0.97 (97%) over the customer-defined evaluation period, measured as the ratio of time the system is capable of performing its intended mission to total operational time, accounting for planned maintenance, unplanned downtime, and safety-driven shutdowns.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-004 is a key performance indicator (KPI) that directly impacts customer satisfaction and mission success. Refining it explicitly incorporates the measurement definition, evaluation period context, and acknowledgment that availability is a system-level property dependent on reliability, maintainability, and safety-driven operational constraints. High safety criticality requires that availability targets not compromise safety; therefore, safety-driven shutdowns are explicitly included in the definition.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Availability shall be verified through continuous telemetry logging of system operational state (ready, operating, maintenance, safety-hold, failed) across the evaluation period. Calculation: Availability = (Total Time − Unplanned Downtime − Safety-Hold Time) / Total Time. Independent audit of logs by customer or third party.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-004.1: The Vehicle subsystem shall achieve Mean Time Between Failures (MTBF) ≥ [target hours] to support system-level availability.
FR-NGHE-004.2: The Teleoperation subsystem shall maintain network link reliability ≥ [target %] to prevent availability loss due to communication failure.
FR-NGHE-004.3: The Safety Services subsystem shall execute safety-driven shutdowns without introducing unplanned downtime; safety holds shall be logged and excluded from availability penalty.
FR-NGHE-004.4: Maintenance procedures shall be designed to minimize Mean Time To Repair (MTTR) and enable predictive/preventive maintenance scheduling.
FR-NGHE-004.5: The NGHE Solution shall provide real-time availability monitoring and alerting to Site Manager and Maintenance stakeholders.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Implement redundancy (hardware and software) across critical subsystems (Vehicle propulsion, Teleoperation network, Safety Services logic) with automatic failover and health monitoring.
DP-B: Design modular architecture with hot-swappable components (battery packs, communication modules, sensor arrays) and rapid replacement procedures to minimize MTTR.
DP-C: Establish predictive maintenance program using telemetry-driven condition monitoring (vibration, temperature, power consumption) to schedule maintenance before failures occur.
DP-D: Implement comprehensive real-time availability dashboard and alerting system that tracks system state, logs downtime events, and notifies stakeholders of availability-impacting conditions.
DP-E: Integrate safety-driven shutdown logic with availability accounting such that safety holds are logged separately and do not penalize availability KPI.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-Integrated: Combine DP-A (redundancy and failover), DP-C (predictive maintenance via telemetry), DP-D (real-time monitoring and alerting), and DP-E (safety-aware availability accounting). This integrated approach addresses both reliability (reducing failures) and maintainability (reducing repair time) while ensuring safety-driven shutdowns are properly accounted for in availability measurement.

potential_sources: Redundancy (DP-A) must be coordinated with Safety Services to ensure failover does not violate safety constraints.
Predictive maintenance (DP-C) depends on Telemetry interface from Vehicle and Teleoperation subsystems; data quality and latency are critical.
Availability monitoring (DP-D) must integrate with Safety Services to distinguish safety-driven shutdowns from failures.
Safety-aware accounting (DP-E) requires clear definition of safety-hold states and logging protocol.
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP is a system-level integration pattern rather than a single design parameter. This is appropriate for a KPI-level FR. Coupling is manageable through well-defined interfaces (Telemetry, SafetyAlert) and clear ownership of each sub-DP across subsystems.

assumptions_risks: ASSUMPTION: Redundancy can be implemented cost-effectively within project budget and weight/power constraints.
ASSUMPTION: Telemetry data from Vehicle and Teleoperation is available with sufficient fidelity and latency (<5 sec) to enable predictive maintenance.
ASSUMPTION: Safety Services can provide clear, real-time indication of safety-hold states for availability accounting.
RISK: Over-reliance on redundancy may increase system complexity and introduce new failure modes (e.g., failover logic bugs).
RISK: Predictive maintenance algorithms may have false-positive or false-negative rates that impact actual availability.
RISK: Availability target of 0.97 may be unachievable given inherent reliability limits of components; customer expectation management required.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP-Integrated by explicitly specifying: (1) redundancy strategy (active-active vs. active-passive, which subsystems are redundant), (2) predictive maintenance thresholds and algorithms, (3) availability dashboard KPIs and alert thresholds, (4) safety-hold logging protocol. These details should be captured in lower-level DPs during decomposition.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: System-level integration module responsible for orchestrating availability across all subsystems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services). Owns the availability monitoring, alerting, and KPI reporting function. Integrates redundancy logic, predictive maintenance scheduling, and safety-aware availability accounting.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives real-time operational state, health metrics (MTBF indicators, temperature, vibration, power), and event logs from Vehicle and Teleoperation subsystems. Used for predictive maintenance and availability monitoring.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Receives safety-driven shutdown signals and safety-hold state notifications from Safety Services. Used to distinguish safety-driven downtime from failures in availability accounting.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Sends availability status, maintenance scheduling recommendations, and failover commands to Vehicle and Teleoperation subsystems. May also send alerts to Site Manager and Maintenance stakeholders.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Communicates availability dashboard, alerts, and KPI reports to Site Manager and Maintenance/Quality stakeholders. May receive configuration updates for availability targets and alert thresholds.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the system-level integration module and is the natural owner of availability KPI orchestration. Availability monitoring, alerting, and KPI reporting are system-level concerns that span all subsystems. Minimal diffusion: only the actual redundancy and predictive maintenance implementations are distributed to Vehicle and Teleoperation, but the coordination and accounting logic resides here.

Vehicle owns its own reliability (MTBF) and maintainability (MTTR) but does not own system-level availability orchestration. Availability is a system property, not a subsystem property. Assigning IM to Vehicle would fragment availability logic across subsystems.
Teleoperation owns network link reliability but not system-level availability. Similar to Vehicle, assigning IM to Teleoperation would create diffusion of availability logic.

Safety Services owns safety-driven shutdown logic but not availability KPI management. Safety and availability are distinct concerns; conflating them in Safety Services would violate separation of concerns.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: NGHE Solution is the appropriate IM boundary because: (1) Availability is a system-level KPI that depends on all subsystems; (2) Availability monitoring, alerting, and KPI reporting are system-level orchestration functions; (3) Safety-aware availability accounting requires a neutral, system-level perspective; (4) Redundancy and failover logic are system-level concerns that coordinate across subsystems.
containment_risks: RISK: NGHE Solution module may become a 'god object' if too many system-level concerns are assigned to it. Mitigation: Clearly scope NGHE Solution to availability orchestration only; other system-level concerns (e.g., mission planning, resource allocation) should be assigned to appropriate subsystems or separate system-level modules.

RISK: Availability monitoring may require real-time processing and low-latency communication; if NGHE Solution is implemented as a centralized software service, network latency or service outage could impact availability itself. Mitigation: Implement availability monitoring with redundancy and failover; consider distributed monitoring with local health checks in Vehicle and Teleoperation.

RISK: Predictive maintenance algorithms may be complex and require domain expertise; if owned by NGHE Solution, this expertise must be available in the system engineering team. Mitigation: Collaborate with Vehicle and Teleoperation subsystem teams to develop and validate algorithms.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Clarify the internal structure of NGHE Solution IM: (1) Define a 'Availability Monitoring' sub-module that owns real-time state tracking and KPI calculation; (2) Define a 'Predictive Maintenance' sub-module that owns condition-based maintenance scheduling; (3) Define an 'Availability Dashboard' sub-module that owns reporting and alerting. These sub-modules should have clear interfaces to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. This structure will reduce the risk of NGHE Solution becoming a monolith.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP (integrated redundancy, predictive maintenance, real-time monitoring, and safety-aware accounting) directly addresses the FR (achieve ≥0.97 availability). The DP is comprehensive and covers both reliability (reducing failures via redundancy and predictive maintenance) and maintainability (reducing repair time via monitoring and alerting). No independence violations detected; the DP components are logically coupled but not contradictory.

diffusion_risks: Redundancy implementation (DP-A) will be distributed across Vehicle and Teleoperation subsystems, but the redundancy orchestration logic (failover, health monitoring) should remain in NGHE Solution IM to avoid diffusion.
Predictive maintenance algorithms (DP-C) will be developed collaboratively with Vehicle and Teleoperation teams, but the scheduling and alerting logic should remain in NGHE Solution IM.
Telemetry data collection (DP-C, DP-D) will be implemented in Vehicle and Teleoperation, but the aggregation and analysis logic should remain in NGHE Solution IM.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution IM is the appropriate boundary for availability orchestration. The IM boundary clearly separates system-level availability concerns (owned by NGHE Solution) from subsystem-level reliability and maintainability concerns (owned by Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services). Interfaces (Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Tasking, Network Link) are well-defined and enable clean separation of concerns.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of FR-NGHE-004 is feasible through continuous telemetry logging and availability calculation. The refined FR includes a clear measurement definition (Availability = (Total Time − Unplanned Downtime − Safety-Hold Time) / Total Time) that can be independently audited. NGHE Solution IM owns the availability monitoring and reporting function, making verification straightforward.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of FR-NGHE-004 to NGHE Solution IM is clear. Sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-004.1 through FR-NGHE-004.5) are allocated to appropriate subsystems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) and will be decomposed further in subsequent levels. The system-level availability KPI is owned by NGHE Solution, while subsystem-level reliability and maintainability KPIs are owned by respective subsystems.

recommended_revisions: Refine the DP to explicitly specify redundancy strategy (which subsystems, active-active vs. active-passive, failover logic).
Refine the DP to specify predictive maintenance thresholds, algorithms, and data requirements.
Refine the DP to specify availability dashboard KPIs, alert thresholds, and reporting frequency.
Refine the DP to specify safety-hold logging protocol and how safety-driven downtime is excluded from availability penalty.
Clarify the internal structure of NGHE Solution IM to avoid it becoming a monolith; define sub-modules for Availability Monitoring, Predictive Maintenance, and Availability Dashboard.
Establish acceptance criteria for availability target (0.97 is a starting point; customer may define different target based on mission criticality and cost-benefit analysis).

decision.justification: FR-NGHE-004 is a system-level KPI that is not atomic and requires decomposition into sub-FRs. The refined FR explicitly identifies five sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-004.1 through FR-NGHE-004.5) that address reliability, maintainability, safety-aware accounting, and monitoring. The selected DP is comprehensive but requires further refinement at lower levels to specify redundancy strategy, predictive maintenance algorithms, and monitoring/alerting logic. The assigned IM (NGHE Solution) is appropriate and well-justified. All gates pass. Proceeding to decompose sub-FRs is the correct next step.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-004.1: The Vehicle subsystem shall achieve Mean Time Between Failures (MTBF) ≥ [target hours] to support system-level availability.
FR-NGHE-004.2: The Teleoperation subsystem shall maintain network link reliability ≥ [target %] to prevent availability loss due to communication failure.
FR-NGHE-004.3: The Safety Services subsystem shall execute safety-driven shutdowns without introducing unplanned downtime; safety holds shall be logged and excluded from availability penalty.
FR-NGHE-004.4: Maintenance procedures shall be designed to minimize Mean Time To Repair (MTTR) and enable predictive/preventive maintenance scheduling.
FR-NGHE-004.5: The NGHE Solution shall provide real-time availability monitoring and alerting to Site Manager and Maintenance stakeholders.

assumptions: Availability target of 0.97 is achievable within project constraints (cost, weight, power, complexity).
Telemetry data from Vehicle and Teleoperation is available with sufficient fidelity and latency (<5 sec) to enable predictive maintenance.
Safety Services can provide clear, real-time indication of safety-hold states for availability accounting.
Redundancy can be implemented without introducing new failure modes that offset reliability gains.
Customer will define evaluation period and acceptance criteria for availability measurement.
Site Manager and Maintenance stakeholders have access to Network Link for receiving availability alerts and dashboard updates.
open_risks: Availability target of 0.97 may be unachievable given inherent reliability limits of components; customer expectation management required.
Over-reliance on redundancy may increase system complexity and introduce new failure modes (e.g., failover logic bugs).
Predictive maintenance algorithms may have false-positive or false-negative rates that impact actual availability.
NGHE Solution IM may become a 'god object' if too many system-level concerns are assigned to it.
Availability monitoring may require real-time processing and low-latency communication; centralized implementation could become a bottleneck or single point of failure.
Safety criticality (high) requires that availability targets not compromise safety; trade-offs between availability and safety must be carefully managed.
evidence_needed: Component-level MTBF data for Vehicle subsystem (propulsion, sensors, actuators).
Network link reliability data for Teleoperation subsystem (latency, packet loss, availability).
Safety Services shutdown response time and reliability data.
Maintenance procedure documentation and MTTR estimates for critical components.
Telemetry data schema and latency specifications from Vehicle and Teleoperation subsystems.
Safety-hold state definitions and logging protocol from Safety Services.
Customer-defined availability target and evaluation period.
Cost-benefit analysis of redundancy options (active-active vs. active-passive, which subsystems).
Predictive maintenance algorithm validation data (false-positive/negative rates, accuracy).
Availability dashboard design and alert threshold specifications.
